tier_profile: Tier-0 flagship review (round-2 targeted reconciliation pass on r1 BLOCKERs + 4 closed advisories)

F-r1-block-1_BCH_regime_partition.verification: F line 980-981 'Schottky-natural Schwarzschild branch. For BH masses M > M_c' (correct); F line 990 'ADW-extremality branch. For M < M_c' (correct). Cross-checked against L3 paper_draft.tex line 127-138 eq:classify (Schwarzschild b.M > M_c, ADWExtremality b.M < M_c) and D3 paper_draft.tex line 747-749 (Schwarzschild branch iff mass exceeds M_c, ADW-extremality otherwise). All three bundles now agree character-for-character. F line 1002-1006 cross-reference summary also agrees ('Schwarzschild branch above M_c, ADW-extremality branch below'). Substrate-register section (sec 10) does not inherit any inversion -- spot-checked surrounding context.

F-r1-block-2_central_charge_factor_2.verification: F line 705-720 sec 5.1 now reads: 'sixteen left-handed Weyl fermions per Standard-Model generation (the 15 chiral fermions plus the right-handed neutrino nu_R, each contributing c = 1/2 to the chiral central charge), the per-generation contribution is c_- = 8, so for N_f generations c_- = 8 N_f.' The framing-anomaly constraint 24 | c_- forces N_f = 0 mod 3 (correct; matches L2 + D2). The Rokhlin 16-convergence is anchored separately at lines 722-727 as Weyl species count (per-generation count of Weyl fermions = 16) NOT as central-charge value. Cross-checked against L2 line 27-29 (c_- = 8 N_f from 16 left-handed Weyl per generation) and L2 line 31 (24 | c_- forcing N_f = 3). Both bundles agree character-for-character.

F-r1-block-3_Wen_non_Abelian_at_sec_10.2.verification: F line 1670-1678 sec 10.2 now reads: 'Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit. ... The deficit closes the emergent-U(1)-string-net-extended-to-gravity channel of Wen at quantitative magnitude (the structurally-distinct emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway is closed separately by gauge erasure; see sec:substrate-register structurally-blocked register).' Phrasing now consistent with F sec 6.2 line 894-895 derivation ('emergent-U(1)-from-string-net construction of Wen extends to gravity'). HOWEVER: the SAME mislabel pattern recurs at F line 176-177 in sec 2.1 NO-GO Results as First-Class Predictive Content (Falsified register list) -- this site was NOT updated by the round-2 fix and still reads 'the Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit closing the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway (D3 sec:gauge-erasure)'. The round-2 patch was applied at the sec 10.2 falsified-register entry but missed the parallel earlier sec 2.1 falsified-register list. New BLOCKER F-r2-block-1 raised to track.

F-r1-adv-1_TheoremNameDrift.verification: Bare 'ratio_to_critical_coupling' string is GONE from paper_draft.tex (grep returns nothing). Only canonical 'HeatKernelExpansion.G_N_sakharov_eq_ratio_critical_coupling' remains at line 953.

F-r1-adv-2_NarrativeOverclaim_first_comp.verification: F line 362 now reads 'eight NO-GO-R5 unanimous --- to our knowledge the first complete-mechanism-family NO-GO closure in Phase 6m'; F line 1349-1351 now reads 'is, to our knowledge, the first complete-mechanism-family unanimous NO-GO closure in Phase 6m'. Both first-claim phrasing sites at sec 2.3 and sec 8.4 are hedged. Note: line 1697 in sec 10 falsified register restates the claim without the hedge -- standard rhetorical pattern (restating an earlier-hedged claim doesn't require re-hedging). Acceptable.

F-r1-adv-3_ProductionRunBacking_WalkerWa.verification: F line 1014-1019 sec 6.5 now reads: 'O(1) constant follows from the substrate's MTC and horizon-boundary structure (its detailed identification with a specific topological-order anomaly-match coefficient is downstream of the substrate's MTC choice and is not the load-bearing content of D3 sec 7).' Walker-Wang attribution removed in favor of MTC-and-horizon-boundary-structure phrasing. WalkerWang2012 bibitem is still cited downstream at line 1156 as part of the [HaydenPreskill, WalkerWang, PastawskiYoshidaHarlowPreskill] collection citation -- this is appropriate (general topological-order citation, not the specific O(1) entropy attribution).

F-r1-adv-4_CrossPaperConsistency_closure.verification: F line 881-886 sec 6.2 now reads: '(D3 sec 4 originally enumerates the closure triad as Wen + fracton + GW170817; F lifts the gauge-erasure prerequisite of D3 sec 3 into the closure list as the structural pre-condition for the substrate-identity claim, and handles the fracton diff-invariance no-go separately as a structurally-blocked register entry in sec:substrate-register.)' Reorganization note explicitly preserves the cross-reading.

F-r1-adv-5_structural_completeness_sec_1.verification: Length-vs-target compression carry-forward; not a r2 fix target. Will be revisited at Stage 13 or expansion-pass time.

F-r1-adv-6_NarrativeOverclaim_E.4_prime.verification: Minor designator inconsistency (E.4 vs E.4-prime) carry-forward; not a r2 fix target. F is consistent on E.1-E.4 throughout the body and was not load-bearing. Acceptable to defer.

blocking_issues.section: F sec 2.1 'NO-GO Results as First-Class Predictive Content' falsified-register list (line 176-177)
blocking_issues.claim_in_F: F line 176-177: 'the Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit closing the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway (D3 sec:gauge-erasure)'.
blocking_issues.siblings_anchor: F sec 6.2 line 894-895 (own internal anchor): 'The emergent-U(1)-from-string-net construction of Wen ... extends to gravity by promoting the photon to a spin-2 graviton through a string-condensate'. F sec 10.2 line 1673-1678 (corrected by r2 patch): 'closes the emergent-U(1)-string-net-extended-to-gravity channel of Wen at quantitative magnitude (the structurally-distinct emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway is closed separately by gauge erasure)'.
blocking_issues.discrepancy: The round-2 patch closed the F-r1-block-3 mislabel at the sec 10.2 falsified-register entry but did NOT update the parallel earlier appearance in the sec 2.1 NO-GO-as-predictive-content falsified-register list at line 176-177. The line 176-177 phrasing is the same logical error as F-r1-block-3: it labels the Wen-ADW deficit as closing the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway when (a) Wen's mechanism is U(1)-string-net to spin-2 (per F's own sec 6.2 + D3 sec 4), and (b) the non-Abelian-emergent-gauge channel is closed structurally by gauge erasure (per F's own sec 6.2 line 891-892), not by the Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit. Additionally the section reference '(D3 sec:gauge-erasure)' at line 177 is incorrect: the Wen-ADW deficit lives in D3 sec 4 'Perturbative Wen-ADW deficit' (D3 paper_draft.tex line 418-436), not in D3's gauge-erasure section. So this is both a mislabel of the closure mechanism AND a misattribution of the D3 section reference -- the same dual-error structurally as F-r1-block-3.
blocking_issues.fix_path: Update F line 176-177 to mirror the corrected sec 10.2 phrasing: replace 'the Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit closing the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway (D3 sec:gauge-erasure)' with 'the Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit closing the emergent-U(1)-string-net-extended-to-gravity channel of Wen at quantitative magnitude (D3 sec 4)'. After fix, grep for any remaining 'non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway' phrasing in connection with Wen-ADW to confirm no third occurrence exists.

non_blocking_followups.issue: Minor designator drift between SYNTHESIS_BRIEF.md ('E.4-prime') and F draft ('E.4'). F is consistent on E.1-E.4 throughout. Not load-bearing.

stage10_round_2_verdict.rationale: AMBER per agent-prompt criterion (1-3 BLOCKERs). All three round-1 BLOCKERs (BCH regime partition F-r1-block-1, central-charge factor-of-2 F-r1-block-2, Wen non-Abelian mislabel at sec 10.2 F-r1-block-3) are CLOSED at the patched sites and cross-verified against L3+D3 (block-1), L2+D2 (block-2), and F's own sec 6.2 (block-3). Four of six round-1 advisories are CLOSED (adv-1 TheoremNameDrift, adv-2 NarrativeOverclaim with 'to our knowledge' hedging at sec 2.3+sec 8.4, adv-3 Walker-Wang attribution rewrite, adv-4 closure-list reorganization note); two carry forward (adv-5 sec 11 length compression, adv-6 E.4 designator) as not load-bearing. ONE NEW BLOCKER F-r2-block-1: the round-2 patch for F-r1-block-3 was applied at F sec 10.2 falsified-register entry (line 1670-1678) but missed the parallel earlier appearance in the F sec 2.1 falsified-register list at line 176-177, which retains the pre-fix mislabel ('Wen-ADW factor-6000 deficit closing the emergent-non-Abelian-gauge-symmetry pathway') AND additionally has an incorrect D3 section reference (the cross-reference points to 'D3 sec:gauge-erasure' but the Wen-ADW deficit lives in D3 sec 4, not the gauge-erasure section). LaTeX compile is clean (PDF 13s newer than tex, 476KB). All 28 spot-checked Lean theorem names resolve in lean_deps.json (the round-1 ratio_to_critical_coupling drift is fixed). 52/52 bibitems still cited and defined with zero undefined.
stage10_round_2_verdict.next_round_gate: Round 3 should be a one-line patch at F line 176-177 to mirror the corrected sec 10.2 phrasing, plus a final grep to ensure no third occurrence of the Wen-non-Abelian-gauge mislabel exists. Estimated round-3 cost: ~1 file edit + ~3 file reads + ~5 tool calls. After round-3 GREEN, F advances to Stage 13 with adv-5 + adv-6 carried forward as Stage-13 advisories.
